title: Soy wax jar candle (rosemary)
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle scented with rosemary essential oil for a herbal, savoury burn
excerpt: A standard 200 ml jar candle in container-grade soy wax with rosemary essential oil at 6% of wax weight. Rosemary is a clean herbal note that pairs with a kitchen or workspace better than a sweet candle. Allow 7 days to cure before the first burn.
sourceNotes: Standard container soy wax pour technique. Rosemary essential oil flash point ~43 degrees Celsius (Tisserand and Young 2014); add at 60-65 degrees Celsius after wax has cooled below pour-up temperature.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (~140 g wax + scent)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by jar count. 500 g wax pellets make 3 candles.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure 7 days before first burn; the rosemary note rounds out over the cure.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 5 years cool, dark, and lidded. Rosemary fades over years but remains recognisable at 2 years. Light surface frosting on soy is normal.

glossaryTerms.definition: The mass of essential oil expressed as a percentage of wax weight. Container candles typically take 6-10% by weight; below 5% the throw is weak, above 10% the wax cannot bind the EO and it weeps out as oily droplets on the surface. Rosemary at 6% sits in the comfortable middle of this range.

content.text: A herbal candle suits a kitchen or a study better than a sweet vanilla. Rosemary essential oil at 6% gives a clear, savoury, slightly camphoraceous note when the candle is lit and a softer infused aroma from the sealed jar. The 200 ml format gives 25-30 hours total burn. The
is conservative compared to a heavy fragrance-oil candle; the rosemary note carries cleanly without sweet overlay.

Pour temperature for soy container wax: 55-58 degrees Celsius. EO addition: 60-65 degrees Celsius (above the rosemary flash point of 43 degrees Celsius, below the wax melt point + 15). Stir 2 full minutes after the EO goes in.

items.prepNote: Container-grade soy wax pellets, melt point 49-52 degrees Celsius.
6% of wax weight. 1,8-cineole rosemary or camphor rosemary both work; camphor is the more medicinal note, 1,8-cineole the brighter.
Pre-tabbed cotton wick rated for 60-70 mm container diameter.

content.text: Touch the wick tab to the bottom centre of the dry jar with a dab of melted wax. Hold 10 seconds. Lay the wick centring bar across the jar top and thread the wick through; it should stand vertical.

Weigh the soy wax into the pouring pitcher. Set in a saucepan with 3 cm simmering water. Stir occasionally until liquid and the thermometer reads 70-75 degrees Celsius. Lift off the water bath and cool to 60-65 degrees Celsius (3-5 minutes).
Add the essential oil and pour

At 60-65 degrees Celsius, weigh in the rosemary essential oil. Stir steadily for 2 full minutes; the EO must fully bond with the wax or it will float out as the candle cools. Cool to 55-58 degrees Celsius. Pour in a single steady stream into the jar, filling to 1.5 cm below the rim. Tap to release bubbles. Rest 1 hour to set.
Cover (lid or baking paper) and cure 7 days in a cool dark cupboard. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
First burn: 2-3 hours to establish a full melt pool. Never leave unattended. Heat-safe surface, away from draughts and curtains. Stop burning when 1 cm wax remains. Trim wick to 5 mm between burns.
Lidded, cool, dark. 5 year shelf life. Light frosting on soy is normal. Rancid smell means discard.
